incidents.vector: Personal data compromise of FBI Director Kash Patel claimed by Iranian hacktivist group linked to IRGC.
incidents.impact: Hackers claim to have exfiltrated personal data; FBI says no government information was taken. State Department reissued $10 million reward for information on the group.

incidents.victim: Government agencies and enterprises worldwide (18,000+ routers)
incidents.vector: DNS hijacking of 18,000+ vulnerable SOHO routers (Mikrotik, TP-Link) to intercept OAuth authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Over 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices compromised. Attackers harvested authentication tokens bypassing MFA, gaining direct access to victim accounts across government ministries, law enforcement, and email providers.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, and financial organizations (US, UK, Australia)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, weaponized days before public disclosure, moving from initial access to ransomware deployment in under 24 hours.
incidents.impact: University of Mississippi Medical Center forced offline for weeks; Passaic County, NJ also hit. Medusa ransomware operators increasingly target healthcare using rapid exploitation and legitimate remote management tools.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack targeting firm employees, enabling unauthorized access to client files.
incidents.impact: Client materials for 10 clients posted online by Silent Ransom Group. Jones Day employs 2,400 lawyers and previously represented the President in election campaigns.

incidents.victim: Open-source software ecosystem (Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, VS Code plug-ins)
incidents.vector: Poisoned GitHub Actions, PyPI packages, and VS Code plug-ins with credential-stealing malware; used compromised access tokens and stolen service accounts to inject infostealers into trusted software distribution channels.
incidents.impact: Hundreds of thousands of credentials potentially stolen. LiteLLM present in 36% of cloud environments. Group also deployed wiper malware targeting Iranian systems. Now reportedly collaborating with LAPSUS$ extortion group.

incidents.vector: Exploited TrueConf video conferencing updater vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) to push malicious updates through a compromised on-premises government server to dozens of connected agencies.
incidents.impact: Dozens of government agencies received weaponized software updates. Attackers deployed ShadowPad malware and the Havoc penetration testing tool via the trusted update channel. CISA ordered US federal agencies to patch by April 16.

incidents.vector: Network intrusion targeting the Uffizi, Palazzo Pitti, and Boboli Gardens IT systems, with ransom demand sent to the museum director's personal phone.
incidents.impact: Photographic server compromised; hackers claimed to have obtained security maps and employee phone data. Museum says nothing was stolen and full backups existed. Incident occurred Feb 1; disclosed publicly Apr 3.

incidents.vector: Wiper attack launched in retaliation for the US-Israel military conflict, targeting manufacturing and distribution systems.
incidents.impact: Order processing, manufacturing, and shipping disrupted for three weeks. Company announced full restoration on April 2. FBI and DHS assisted recovery.

incidents.vector: Italian surveillance company ASIGINT (subsidiary of SIO) distributed fake WhatsApp apps rigged with spyware to targeted victims.
incidents.impact: Approximately 200 users tricked into installing spyware. Second Meta disruption of spyware activity in Italy in 15 months, following the Paragon spyware scandal in early 2025.

incidents.victim: Axios NPM package users (100M+ weekly downloads)
incidents.vector: Social engineering of Axios maintainer via fake company and video call. Compromised account used to inject malicious dependency deploying cross-platform WAVESHAPER.V2 backdoor.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions of the world's most popular JavaScript HTTP library served for ~3 hours. Backdoor deployed to Windows, macOS, and Linux, enabling remote access, file enumeration, and payload execution. Malicious versions removed.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.1) in FortiClient EMS, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted requests. Exploitation began Easter weekend.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed by CISA and Singapore government. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks. Federal agencies given days to apply emergency hotfix.

incidents.victim: Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway customers
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler, enabling unauthenticated attackers to read sensitive memory from enterprise network gateways.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed within days of patch release. Compared to previous CitrixBleed incidents that impacted hundreds of hospitals and critical infrastructure organizations. CISA ordered emergency federal patching.

incidents.vector: Operatives posed as a quantitative trading firm at a crypto conference, deposited $1M to build trust, then compromised staff devices via malicious Apple TestFlight and VS Code extensions to multi-sign fraudulent transactions.
incidents.impact: $285 million stolen from Drift's treasury. Attack linked to North Korean group that previously targeted cryptocurrency platforms.

incidents.vector: Breach of the European Commission's Amazon Web Services account, detected and blocked on March 24.
incidents.impact: Internal data potentially stolen. Investigation ongoing to determine extent of breach. Second cyber incident affecting Commission staff in recent months.

incidents.vector: Targeting of messaging apps (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger) through phishing, QR codes, and device-linking tricks to access accounts of government officials and high-risk individuals.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint advisory warning of growing Russian-origin attacks against messaging apps. Previous campaigns linked to FSB and IRGC targeting government officials globally.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities and VPNs lacking MFA, using intermittent encryption to move from initial access to full data encryption in under one hour.
incidents.impact: Akira has collected at least $245 million in ransom payments since 2023. FBI and CISA flagged the group as one of the top ransomware threats, primarily targeting SMBs in manufacturing, education, healthcare, and financial sectors.
